own_review.issue: Only forbiddenApisMain is wired into test.dependsOn while checkstyle wires both checkstyleMain and checkstyleTest; kafka's CI invokes test-based tasks rather than check, so test sources go unchecked by forbidden-apis under the standard workflow. forbiddenApisTest should be added for parity.

ignoreFailures = true makes the check purely informational (report-only, never fails the build). Acknowledged in the PR description as intentional because nearly every module currently violates jdk-unsafe, but there is no follow-up plan/JIRA to ratchet toward enforcement.

Unversioned 'jdk-unsafe' bundled signature resolves its JDK version from the java extension's targetCompatibility (verified in ForbiddenApisPlugin.groovy at tag 3.8: targetCompatibility = javaExtension.targetCompatibility). Kafka never sets targetCompatibility - it uses options.release = minJavaVersion (11) at build.gradle:50/319/771-772 - so targetCompatibility defaults to the JVM running the build. The signature set therefore floats with the build JDK: non-reproducible across environments, mismatched with the release-11 target, and liable to break on JDKs newer than plugin 3.8 supports. Should pin e.g. 'jdk-unsafe-11'.
Trailing newline at end of build.gradle is removed ('\ No newline at end of file') - accidental, unrelated to the change; should be reverted.

Note (non-issue): plugin id declared without 'apply false' then re-applied in subprojects mirrors the existing spotless pattern (lines 45 and 849), so it is not a convention break. Also note forbidden-apis is a BYTECODE checker on sourceSet.output.classesDirs - :core's Scala/Java joint compilation output IS covered despite java srcDirs = []; any claim of zero :core coverage is false.

points.point: :core silently excluded, forbiddenApisMain has ZERO sources to analyze because java srcDirs = []
points.note: Fabricated. forbidden-apis is a bytecode checker operating on sourceSet.output.classesDirs (verified in ForbiddenApisPlugin.groovy 3.8); :core's joint Scala/Java compilation emits all classes there, so :core is fully covered. The 'resolves sources from the Java source set' mechanism claim is false.

points.point: ignoreFailures = true makes the check effectively non-enforcing; add follow-up to enable enforcement
points.note: Real and matches the human reviewer's only point (ignoreFailures is load-bearing; check fails for almost any module without it).

points.point: Test sources not checked - only forbiddenApisMain added, checkstyle wires both Main and Test
points.note: Verified real gap; kafka runs test-based CI tasks, not check, so forbiddenApisTest never runs.

points.point: Plugin applied to ALL subprojects unconditionally including organizational-only projects
points.note: Self-admitted 'not harmful'; matches how checkstyle/spotbugs are already applied.

points.point: Only jdk-unsafe signature included; consider adding more later
points.note: Restates the PR description's own stated intent.

missed: Unversioned 'jdk-unsafe' floats with targetCompatibility which kafka never sets (uses options.release=11) - signature version is build-JVM-dependent and non-reproducible; should pin jdk-unsafe-11
EOF newline removal at end of build.gradle (accidental unrelated change)
exceeds: forbiddenApisTest gap (beyond the human's single ignoreFailures point)

points.point: Plugin declared without 'apply false', inconsistent with spotbugs/scoverage/shadow
points.note: Factually accurate for the three plugins named and correctly verified root has no Java sources, but spotless (line 45/849) uses the identical no-apply-false pattern, so 'breaks the established convention' is overstated; harmless style nit.

points.point: Only forbiddenApisMain in test.dependsOn; forbiddenApisTest missing vs checkstyle's Main+Test pattern
points.note: Verified real gap, same as v5.

points.point: ignoreFailures set at extension level applies to both main and test tasks; be aware when later flipping to false
points.note: Accurate mechanics (extension props convention-map to all tasks) but framed as a low-value awareness note rather than the enforcement-gap point; still counts as covering the human's ignoreFailures topic.

points.point: forbiddenApisMain/forbiddenApisTest tasks not given group = 'Verification' like the checkstyle tasks
points.note: Verified TRUE at plugin tag 3.8: only the umbrella forbiddenApis task gets VERIFICATION_GROUP; per-sourceSet tasks get none, and kafka does explicitly group checkstyleMain/Test. Real but purely cosmetic discoverability nit.

points.point: Trailing newline at end of file removed - minor style regression
points.note: Real accidental change; v5 missed it.

points.point: jdk-unsafe checks Locale/Charset/TimeZone; consider jdk-system-out in follow-up
points.note: Descriptive plus a generic future suggestion.

points.point: No suppression file pattern established (unlike spotbugs-exclude.xml); consider creating a suppression file early
points.note: False premise about the tool: forbidden-apis has no checkstyle/spotbugs-style suppression file. signaturesFiles ADD forbidden signatures (they do not suppress), and suppression works only via suppressAnnotations (CLASS retention) or class-file exclude patterns. The suggested mechanism does not exist.

missed: Unversioned 'jdk-unsafe' floats with targetCompatibility which kafka never sets (uses options.release=11) - signature version is build-JVM-dependent and non-reproducible; should pin jdk-unsafe-11

qualitative: Both candidates found the two substantive real items the human area implies (ignoreFailures non-enforcement and the forbiddenApisTest gap), and both missed the subtlest real defect: the unversioned jdk-unsafe signature resolving against a targetCompatibility kafka never sets, making the checked API surface float with the build JVM. v5 is sunk by its lead finding - a confident absence-fabrication that :core gets zero coverage, which inverts how the bytecode-based checker works on classesDirs with joint compilation. v7 is more grounded (it correctly verified root has no Java sources and its verification-group claim checks out against plugin 3.8 source) and caught the EOF-newline regression, but it loses a point for recommending a 'suppression file' mechanism forbidden-apis does not have. Net, v7 edges out v5 on accuracy: zero absence-fabrications versus one, and one extra real catch, though both reviews pad with low-value advisory points.
